title: The Rise of Nearshoring: Why Companies Are Moving Production Closer to Home - Global Trade Magazine
snippet: Shipping expenses, customs delays, ... and disruption risks can reduce the savings of distant manufacturing. Nearshoring often creates a stronger overall cost balance. Lower transport costs, faster replenishment, and fewer delays can offset higher production expenses. Companies are increasingly evaluating full supply chain economics rather than focusing only on factory labor rates. Technology is strengthening the nearshoring model. Logistics Automation ...
